breaking.title: E Jean Carroll receives $5.6 million in sexual abuse case after years-long legal battle with Trump
breaking.summary: Writer E. Jean Carroll has received over $5.6 million in damages awarded in her civil case against US President Donald Trump after a federal court released funds held in escrow during the appeals process. The payment follows the US Supreme Court's decision to let the 2023 verdict stand. Trump continues to challenge the ruling and has filed further appeals.

breaking.title: US Halts Immigration Traffic Stops After 2 Fatal Shootings
breaking.summary: Last week, an ICE agent shot dead Lorenzo Salgado, a 52-year-old Mexican, during an operation in Texas.

breaking.title: Police say Ann Widdecombe killed in 'targeted attack' as motive investigated
breaking.summary: Counter terror police say they have obtained a warrant to hold a 28-year-old suspect for up to seven days.

breaking.title: Government summons Iran envoy after Indian national killed in attack on ship
breaking.summary: External Affairs Ministry spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said the Indian side conveyed “deepest concerns” about the incident in the Strait of Hormuz and urged for “de-escalation”

breaking.title: Heavy rain collapses roof and kills 11 people in northwest Pakistan
breaking.summary: Officials in northwest Pakistan say 11 people were killed overnight when heavy rain caused the roof of a mud-brick house to collapse

breaking.title: Israeli strike on police post in north Gaza kills seven, officials say
breaking.summary: A senior officer in the Hamas-run police force was among those killed in the strike, which Israel's military says targeted "terrorists".

breaking.title: Supreme Court says call records alone not cogent proof, clears woman of husband’s murder
breaking.summary: Mere production of telephone records does not substitute substantive proof of an illicit affair leading to murder, the top court said in the case dating back to 2007

breaking.title: Incentives, warnings and assurances: how an Indian sailor transited Hormuz
breaking.summary: Indian seafarer aboard oil tanker recalls incentives from his employer to make the risky transit via route near Oman, the threats from the Iranian side to stop, and encouragement from the U.S. Navy to move ahead
